name: Tribal Arts Zion
description: Native American art and jewelry sourced directly from tribal artists.
highlights: Authentic Native American art and jewelry
Sourced directly from tribal artists
A meaningful cultural stop in Springdale

name: David J. West Gallery
description: Fine art photography of the Southwest in light that makes you question whether you've ever really seen these places.
highlights: Fine art photography of the Southwest
Extraordinary light and perspective
A quiet, contemplative stop

name: Springdale Farmers Market
description: Saturday mornings in season. Local produce, artisan goods.
highlights: Saturday mornings in season
Local produce and artisan goods
A window into the community
seasonalNotes: Seasonal — Saturday mornings, typically spring through fall.

name: Paiute Cultural Heritage
location: Cedar City, UT — Paiute Indian Tribe of Utah
description: The Southern Paiute called this land Mukuntuweap long before it was Zion. The Paiute Indian Tribe of Utah preserves language, oral history, and traditions through cultural programs and the annual Restoration Powwow in Cedar City each June.
highlights: The land's original name: Mukuntuweap
Language preservation and oral history programs
Annual Restoration Powwow in Cedar City each June
Supporting their programs is the most meaningful reciprocity you can offer this place

name: Pipe Spring National Monument
location: Fredonia, AZ — jointly managed by NPS and Kaibab Band of Paiutes
description: A desert oasis that tells the layered story of water, sovereignty, and survival — Native, pioneer, and ranching history in one place. The Kaibab Paiutes operate the visitor center.
highlights: Jointly managed by NPS and the Kaibab Band of Paiutes
A desert oasis with layered history
Kaibab Paiutes operate the visitor center
Water, sovereignty, and survival in one place

name: Zion Forever Project
description: The park's official nonprofit partner. Conservation volunteer days, trail restoration, hanging garden protection, and dark sky preservation. A way to give back to the land that gives so much.
highlights: Official nonprofit partner of Zion National Park
Trail restoration and wildlife habitat protection
Dark sky stewardship and youth education
Volunteer opportunities available

name: Conserve Southwest Utah
location: Red Cliffs NCA, near St. George — 45 min from Zion
description: Hands-on desert habitat restoration at Red Cliffs NCA. Planting native shrubs, protecting threatened Mojave desert tortoise habitat, invasive species removal. Over 5,000 native plants restored since 2020.
highlights: Hands-on desert habitat restoration
Mojave desert tortoise protection
Over 5,000 native plants restored since 2020
Regular volunteer days

name: Parowan Gap Petroglyphs
description: A free, open-air gallery of ancient rock art attributed to the Fremont people. Hundreds of petroglyphs etched into the canyon walls — a contemplative stop that asks nothing but attention.
highlights: Free, open-air gallery of ancient rock art
Attributed to the Fremont people
Hundreds of petroglyphs etched into canyon walls
Self-guided, contemplative

name: Fruita Orchards at Capitol Reef
description: The park's historic orchard — apricot, cherry, peach, pear, apple — is still harvested by visitors in season. Walk in, pick fruit off the tree, pay by the pound. One of the most quietly extraordinary things you can do in any national park.
highlights: Historic orchard with U-pick fruit in season
Apricot, cherry, peach, pear, apple
Pay by the pound — free to enter
One of the most special NPS experiences anywhere
